subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistently foregrounds the key concept of change throughout key sections
Demonstrates clear analytical thinking linking change to costs, capacity and stakeholder relations
Strong organizationâspecific analysis grounded in Pelm & Repaâs context
weaknesses: Integration lapses in some financial sections with minimal conceptual linkage
Does not fully explain why âchangeâ is central to the research question
Could apply stakeholder theory more deeply when discussing displaced workers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission exhibits a strong, organizationâspecific understanding of the key concept of change, meeting the descriptor for Band 4. While change is clearly analyzed in the introduction and conclusion, several financial analyses revert to numeric focus without explicitly tying back to the concept. This partial but not pervasive integration precludes the top band.

strengths: Includes four supporting documents meeting the 3â5 document requirement
Documents are relevant and support various facets (ethics, customer survey, financial evidence)
Adequate range of sources provides necessary context
weaknesses: Depth of some documents is limited (e.g., website adds little analytical value)
Missing citations/footnotes and incomplete figure/table captions reduce clarity
Empty placeholder table suggests incomplete compilation of materials
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With four relevant documents, the IA meets the quantitative requirement and provides adequate support, consistent with Band 3 for relevance and depth. However, uneven depth and referencing weaknesses prevent it from achieving the highest band.

strengths: Selects recognized financial tools (ARR, payback period) alongside conceptual analysis of change
Tools are applied directly to the research question to inform a recommendation
Demonstrates understanding of basic application mechanics
weaknesses: Toolkit lacks breadth (e.g., no SWOT, break-even or force-field analysis)
Some calculation workings are inconsistent or mislabeled
Non-financial impacts are only briefly connected back to theoretical frameworks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student makes an adequate selection and application of tools and theories, yielding insights that inform decisionâmaking. Inconsistencies in calculations and limited breadth constrain the work to Band 3 rather than the top band of effective, fully integrated selection and application.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses sufficient financial data (forecasts, cost savings) and stakeholder survey results
Presents data clearly in tables and applies it in comparative analyses
Analysis is mostly effective, linking evidence to the decision and acknowledging qualitative factors
weaknesses: Reasoning sometimes remains superficial, with unexplained accounting conventions
Errors in ARR annualization and unaddressed electricity cost trade-offs
Limited discussion of strategies to mitigate identified limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates effective data selection and mostly effective analysis, matching the Band 4 descriptors for this criterion. Minor errors in methodology and occasional superficial reasoning prevent the sustained, thorough integration required for Band 5.

strengths: Conclusions recommend machinery purchase based on higher ARR and shorter payback
Explicitly answers the research question regarding dumpling machinery
weaknesses: Calculation inconsistencies and limited treatment of non-financial risks reduce overall reliability
Conclusion is somewhat generic and could more tightly tie findings back to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion clearly answers the research question, inconsistencies in evidence and underdeveloped non-financial risk analysis align with Band 2 for partly consistent conclusions. The work does not reach the fully consistent synthesis of Band 3.

strengths: Clear table of contents and logical sectioning guide the reader
Coherent flow of ideas demonstrates appropriate structure throughout
weaknesses: Occasional formatting issues (duplicated headings, uneven table labels)
Missing page numbers reduce navigability in the contents
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA maintains an appropriate, wellâorganized structure throughout, meriting the maximum of Band 2. Minor formatting errors do not detract from the overall coherence.

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements (title, contents, word count, appendices, references)
Word count is clearly provided, demonstrating adherence to IA requirements
weaknesses: Inconsistent table and figure labels, missing units and captions hinder clarity
Bibliography entries lack a unified citation style
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required elements are present, satisfying the top band for presentation. Formatting inconsistencies are minor and do not undermine the inclusion of necessary IA components.

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear table of contents, logical sectioning and coherent flow demonstrate an **appropriate structure throughout**. Only occasional formatting issues (duplicated headings, uneven table labelling) are present, so Band 2 is warranted.

Each document is **relevant**âthe interview informs ethics, the survey gauges customer reaction, and the financial file provides numerical evidenceâyet depth varies (e.g., survey sample limitations acknowledged, website offers little analytical value). Collectively they provide adequate support but not the wide-ranging, in-depth insight required for the top band.
The IA includes all required elements for HL: title, contents page, word count, appendices, references and supporting documents. Therefore it meets the top presentation band.

Four supporting documents are listed (interview, website, internal financial document, survey). This meets the 3-5 document expectation but does not exceed it or offer exceptional breadth. Band 2 therefore applies for the ânumber of documentsâ strand.

The chosen tools are applied directly to the research question; calculations of ARR and payback lead to a recommendation. However, some workings appear inconsistent (e.g., contribution figures given as negatives but interpreted as cost reductions) and non-financial impacts are only briefly linked back to tools. Application is therefore adequate but not consistently effectiveâBand 3 fits best.

The report draws on **sufficient financial data** (six-month forecasts, salary savings, energy costs) and stakeholder information (survey percentages). Data is clearly presented in numerous tables and applied to calculations, meeting the descriptor for Band 4. Minor errors and a limited range of qualitative data prevent the âeffectiveâ standard of Band 5.

Analysis links the data to the decision, comparing ARR and payback for two machines and acknowledging stakeholder ethics. Nonetheless, reasoning is sometimes superficial (e.g., cost reductions claimed without reconciling negative contributions) and integration of qualitative factors is brief. This matches âsome analysis of research questionââBand 3.

The student **explicitly answers the research question**, recommending the shift to dumpling machinery while discussing costs, ethics and customer concerns, satisfying the highest descriptor for this strand.

The student selects several recognised toolsâpayback period, ARR, scatter-plot line of best fit, and conceptual analysis of change. While these constitute an **adequate** toolkit, more breadth (SWOT, break-even, force-field, etc.) would be expected for âeffectiveâ selection. Thus Band 3 is appropriate.

The conclusion that dumpling machinery should be purchased aligns broadly with the higher ARR and shorter payback evidenced. Yet calculation inconsistencies and scant treatment of non-financial risks weaken full consistency. Hence âsome conclusions consistent with evidenceâ (Band 2).

Throughout the report, financial figures, staffing data and primary research are explicitly tied to Pelm & Repaâs operations, demonstrating a **strong organisation-specific analysis**. The survey, interview and internal financial document all anchor the discussion in the firmâs real context. Nevertheless, some general statements on market growth and economies of scale are treated generically without exploring how this particular six-outlet chain would operationalise them, preventing fully seamless integration. Hence Band 4 is most fitting.

The student consistently foregrounds the key concept of **change**, explaining how a move from handmade to machine production would impact costs, capacity, labour and stakeholder relations. References to ethical implications for displaced workers and the strategic opportunity to exploit economies of scale show a clear conceptual grasp that goes beyond a simple definition. However, integration is strongest in the introduction and conclusion while several financial sections revert to a purely numeric focus with little linkage back to the broader idea of organisational transformation. This partial but not pervasive integration aligns with Band 4.

annotations.comment: Table 2âs parentheses indicate negative contribution without explanation; clarify the accounting convention used.
Table 3 (after machinery) is empty; ensure data is fully populated or remove placeholder.
The summary after Tables 2 and 3 describes trends but doesnât reference specific figures; strengthen analysis with data citations.
Payback description introduces the tool but should tie cash flows back to the dumpling sales forecast for coherence.
The payback table shows positive cumulative cash flow in month 2 but text states 1 month 12 days; reconcile this inconsistency.
The student correctly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept but should explain why this concept is central to the research question to strengthen integration.
The research question is clear but could be more specific by indicating a time frame or quantifying the cost savings sought.
Word count is provided, demonstrating adherence to IA formatting requirements.
The table of contents covers all major sections, but page numbers should be added to improve navigation.
Consider adding document page numbers next to supporting documents for clearer referencing in the appendices.
Introduction clearly describes Pelm & Repa, but it would benefit from explicitly connecting these facts to the concept of change.
Figure 1 gives visual context but the caption lacks source information and date; include those details for completeness.
You note there are 12 people in production but donât tie this baseline into your change scenario; elaborate on current operations.
The discussion of Estonian market growth is valuable; strengthen analysis by comparing projected growth to companyâs current performance.
Figure 2 lacks axis titles and units, making interpretation of revenue growth unclear; add these for clarity.
The sentence on stakeholder coordination ends mid-thought (âone whoâ); ensure full sentences for coherence.
Ethical implications are introduced but would benefit from applying stakeholder theory to assess impacts in depth.
Methodology describes sampling ages 18â40 but doesnât justify this range; explain why this demographic was chosen.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The financial position section uses data from an internal document but lacks footnote citation; include a footnote for transparency.
Table 1 (Dumplings) lacks a table number and title; add consistent labels to all tables for clarity.
Analysis states salaries drop but omits discussion of increased electricity costs; discuss this trade-off explicitly.
Bibliography entries lack consistent formatting; adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) throughout.
Including payback periods by dumpling type is useful; consider integrating these results with your ARR analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The PBP formula is repeated; streamline the presentation by showing it clearly once before applying it.
ARR calculation divides cumulative return by 6 months rather than annualizing; convert to a yearly basis for accuracy.
The concluding sentence about performance is generic; explicitly tie the outcome back to your research question.
Table 5 caption appears above the table, but IA conventions place captions below; adjust placement.
The pancake cost analysis overlooks fixed electricity costsâ impact on profitability; discuss this additional cost.
Pancake payback table extends to month 9; explain the extrapolation method and assumptions.
Figure 3âs axes are unlabeled and there is no trendline equation; add labels and equation to improve interpretability.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The text mentions using a line of best fit for prediction but does not discuss its limitations or validity.
Conclusion paragraph is cut off and runs into the next page; ensure a complete, coherent ending.
The summary of limitations is insightful but should propose strategies to mitigate these issues.
